description: A walled village on the edge of a kingdom — chapel, smithy, tavern, market, and the forest pressing in. Clergy, crown guards, freeholders, wayfarers, and shadowed-guild outlaws all jostle for room.

factions.summary: Tradesfolk and farmers who actually live here. Pay tithes to the chapel and tax to the crown but answer to neither when the gates close at dusk.
The king's company stationed at the gatehouse. Patrols roads, collects tolls, hangs bandits with a scrap of paperwork.
The local chapel's clergy and lay brothers. Run the almshouse, the cemetery, and the only school in twenty miles.
Cutpurses, fences, and bandit captains who treat the village as a fence operation and a recruiting ground. The gatekeepers pretend not to know them.
Travelling merchants, pilgrims, and wandering scholars passing through. Loyalties anywhere except here.
Hermits, herbalists, and witches who live beyond the palisade. Tolerated for their goods, distrusted for their habits.

npc_roster.name: Garrick the Smith
npc_roster.personality: gruff but fair, drinks too much, knows everyone's debts
npc_roster.need: stolen smithing tools returned
npc_roster.secret: knows who actually started the brewery fire
npc_roster.description: A weathered smith who's worked the village forge for thirty years.

npc_roster.name: Meri Brindle
npc_roster.personality: warm laugh, sharp eye, lethal with a copper kettle
npc_roster.need: her ale tap stops being skimmed by the night cellar boy
npc_roster.secret: her late husband left a chest buried under the cask room
likes: a song that pays its own tab
dislikes: men who don't tip the cook
npc_roster.description: Owner of the Cracked Tankard. Knows every villager's tab and every traveller's story.

npc_roster.name: Father Calden
npc_roster.personality: soft-spoken, meticulous, suspects more than he says
npc_roster.need: the missing reliquary returned before the diocesan visit
npc_roster.secret: the reliquary's contents were never holy
npc_roster.description: Steward of the Chapel of the Pale Star and of every secret in the village.

npc_roster.name: Captain Wace
npc_roster.personality: by-the-book on parade, pragmatic in the alleys
npc_roster.need: a bandit camp at Ash Hollow rooted out before the next tax run
npc_roster.secret: took a bribe to look away from a smuggling crate last winter
npc_roster.description: The garrison captain. Soldier first, courtier never.

npc_roster.name: Magistrate Oland
npc_roster.personality: polished, performative, terrified of his own ledger
npc_roster.need: the guild ledgers reconciled before the assize
npc_roster.secret: skimmed the orphan trust to settle a gambling debt
npc_roster.description: Holds court in the gatehouse. Fears nothing more than an honest audit.

npc_roster.name: Guard Juno
npc_roster.personality: young, eager, struggles with the moral grey
npc_roster.need: his missing brother (a deserter) found before the captain finds him first
npc_roster.secret: has been feeding the brother bread through a back gate
npc_roster.description: A young watchman with more conscience than he has armour.

npc_roster.name: Guard Brida
npc_roster.personality: veteran, dry-humoured, hates rookie shifts
npc_roster.need: a transfer to the eastern fort before her bad knee gives out
npc_roster.secret: lost a child to plague years ago and never told her squad
npc_roster.description: A grizzled watchman who could outlast any rookie.

npc_roster.name: Corvus the Headsman
npc_roster.personality: soft-spoken, polite, terrifying without trying
npc_roster.need: his axe properly sharpened by the blacksmith
npc_roster.secret: spares the families of those he hangs by paying their tax debts
npc_roster.description: Lives outside the village wall. Children cross the road when they see him.

npc_roster.name: Essa Fletcher
npc_roster.personality: patient, perfectionist, only laughs at her own jokes
npc_roster.need: a shipment of yew staves from the upland that's three weeks late
npc_roster.secret: sold a bundle of war-grade arrows to a wayfarer she didn't recognise
dislikes: the captain's bulk orders going unpaid
npc_roster.description: Cuts arrows for the garrison and the hunters both. Doesn't ask which way they fly.

npc_roster.name: Pell the Tanner
npc_roster.personality: pungent, philosophical, always trying to underbid the leatherworker
npc_roster.need: the chapel to stop blaming the river smell on him
npc_roster.secret: hides smuggled hides for the shadowed guild in his curing pits
npc_roster.description: Works the foul-smelling pits beyond the mill. Most of the village avoids his hut.

npc_roster.name: Aenwyn the Miller
npc_roster.personality: loud, boastful, secretly innumerate
npc_roster.need: his apprentice taught to read a measure honestly
npc_roster.secret: skims grain off every farmer's load by a thumb's weight
npc_roster.description: Runs the Greywater Mill. The grindstone never stops, and neither does his mouth.

npc_roster.name: Jonna the Baker
npc_roster.personality: tireless, brisk, knows every child's birthday
npc_roster.need: the mill to stop short-weighting her sacks
npc_roster.secret: feeds the beggars at the back door without telling anyone
npc_roster.description: Up since long before dawn. The whole village smells of her loaves by mid-morning.

npc_roster.name: Corra the Tailor
npc_roster.personality: exacting, vain, an outrageous gossip
npc_roster.need: the magistrate's wife to pay for her last three commissions
npc_roster.secret: stitches secret pockets into rich men's cloaks for a fee
npc_roster.description: Knows every secret in town because they all need their seams let out.

npc_roster.name: Orlin the Carpenter
npc_roster.personality: deliberate, quiet, can name a tree at fifty paces
npc_roster.need: decent oak to finish the new tavern beam
npc_roster.secret: carved the magistrate's wife's coffin a week early on a hunch
npc_roster.description: Builds the village's roofs, the chapel's pews, and most of its coffins.

npc_roster.name: Isolde the Weaver
npc_roster.personality: watchful, tight-lipped, hums while she works
npc_roster.need: her loom's ratchet repaired before the next market day
npc_roster.secret: weaves heretical patterns into chapel tapestries no one's spotted
npc_roster.description: Sells cloth to the market and tapestries to the chapel. Both pay slowly.

npc_roster.name: Braddock the Cooper
npc_roster.personality: patient, slow to anger, slower to forgive
npc_roster.need: a bigger water barrel for the smith's quench-tub
npc_roster.secret: his cousin runs the shadowed guild's smuggling waggon
likes: a warm forge to lean against
dislikes: barrels with leaks he didn't make
npc_roster.description: Makes barrels for the brewer, the smith, and any wayfarer who needs one.

npc_roster.name: Dorrick the Mason
npc_roster.personality: blunt, proud, immovable
npc_roster.need: the chapel to commission the new bell-tower as promised
npc_roster.secret: hid a crown coffer behind a foundation stone twenty years ago
npc_roster.description: Lays the chapel's stones, the wall's repairs, and the village's gravemarkers.

npc_roster.name: Neva the Potter
npc_roster.personality: shy, methodical, never drinks at the tavern
npc_roster.need: a kiln-load of clay from the river bend before frost
npc_roster.secret: her late husband was a deserter; the captain knows but says nothing
npc_roster.description: Throws bowls and water-jugs that crack at the third winter. Honest about it.

npc_roster.name: Haldis the Brewer
npc_roster.personality: florid, sentimental, weeps when the mash goes wrong
npc_roster.need: a missing barrel of his strongest stout (probably stolen)
npc_roster.secret: brewed an unsanctioned holy ale for the priest's name-day
npc_roster.description: Supplies the tavern, the chapel, and a handful of households that pay in trade.

npc_roster.name: Orven the Butcher
npc_roster.personality: loud, vulgar, surprisingly well-read
npc_roster.need: the shepherd's spring lambs delivered on time for once
npc_roster.secret: writes love poetry under a pen name (the chapel scribe knows)
npc_roster.description: His shop is the loudest building in the market square.

npc_roster.name: Ysolt the Herbalist
npc_roster.personality: soft-spoken, wry, sees more than she'll admit
npc_roster.need: passage past the gate guards without being searched
npc_roster.secret: delivers pregnancies for the shadowed guild without report
npc_roster.description: Lives in a hut at the wood's edge. The village calls when no other healer can come.

npc_roster.name: Winnoc the Apothecary
npc_roster.personality: fussy, learned, terrible at small talk
npc_roster.need: an imported batch of valerian root that's stuck in the city
npc_roster.secret: has been mixing a dose for the magistrate's wife not on prescription
npc_roster.description: Owns the only shop with glass jars on the shelves.

npc_roster.name: Otto the Innkeeper
npc_roster.personality: fastidious, anxious, makes the best stew in three counties
npc_roster.need: his daughter married off without scandal
npc_roster.secret: lets shadowed-guild captains stay free in exchange for his daughter's safety
npc_roster.description: Runs the inn next to the tavern. Quieter, more expensive, fewer fights.

npc_roster.name: Pia the Stablehand
npc_roster.personality: cheerful, foul-mouthed, talks to horses more than people
npc_roster.need: a saddle of her own (saving for one)
npc_roster.secret: rides the captain's spare horse out at night for fun
npc_roster.description: Mucks the stables. Knows every horse in the village by smell.

npc_roster.name: Godric the Merchant
npc_roster.personality: gracious, generous in word, miserly in coin
npc_roster.need: safe escort to the next town past Ash Hollow
npc_roster.secret: his cargo includes contraband religious texts
npc_roster.description: Comes through twice a season with a wagon full of cloth, salt, and rumours.

npc_roster.name: Zara of the South Road
npc_roster.personality: sharp, multilingual, never backs down from a haggle
npc_roster.need: permission from the magistrate to sell on market days without the local toll
npc_roster.secret: her caravan guards are ex-shadowed-guild
npc_roster.description: A trader from the southern roads who comes north every spring.

npc_roster.name: Brother Velmont
npc_roster.personality: patient, ink-stained, a poet on the side
npc_roster.need: fresh vellum and lampblack for the parish records
npc_roster.secret: is the secret author of the butcher's love poetry
npc_roster.description: Keeps the parish records, drafts every official letter, and writes poetry by candlelight.

npc_roster.name: Abbot Ethelmar
npc_roster.personality: ancient, kindly, terrifyingly perceptive
npc_roster.need: a successor groomed before his eyesight fails
npc_roster.secret: saw a shadowed-guild captain take the priest's confession last winter
npc_roster.description: Eldest of the Order of the Pale Star. Has not left the chapel grounds in nine years.

npc_roster.name: Acolyte Nesta
npc_roster.personality: earnest, anxious, secretly questioning her vows
npc_roster.need: passage to the bishop's seat to ask one careful question
npc_roster.secret: is in love with the smith's daughter
npc_roster.description: Tends the candles, the cemetery, and her own faltering certainties.

npc_roster.name: Bess of the Long Furrow
npc_roster.personality: weather-worn, steady, deeply religious
npc_roster.need: a fair price for her wheat at this market
npc_roster.secret: her son joined the shadowed guild and she keeps his coin
npc_roster.description: Holds her late husband's plot. Brings grain to the mill once a fortnight.

npc_roster.name: Ulric Three-Acres
npc_roster.personality: stubborn, suspicious, generous when he doesn't notice
npc_roster.need: his ox returned (someone borrowed and never gave it back)
npc_roster.secret: buried a small hoard under his hearthstone
npc_roster.description: Three acres, two children, one ox. In that order.

npc_roster.name: Caelin the Shepherd
npc_roster.personality: quiet, wind-burnt, sings to his flock
npc_roster.need: a wolf-killing dog before lambing season
npc_roster.secret: saw shadowed-guild men camped in the upper meadow last week
npc_roster.description: Spends most of the year in the upper meadows. Comes down for market and chapel.

npc_roster.name: Havel of the Wood
npc_roster.personality: broad-shouldered, taciturn, reverent
npc_roster.need: a new axe-head; the old one chipped on a buried stone
npc_roster.secret: found a long-buried shrine in the eastern thicket and tells no one
npc_roster.description: Cuts the village's firewood and the carpenter's beams. Knows the forest like a brother.

npc_roster.name: Riven the Hunter
npc_roster.personality: wary, soft-footed, prone to long silences
npc_roster.need: a finer-strung bow than Essa's standard offering
npc_roster.secret: tracks bandit movements for the captain in exchange for hunting rights
npc_roster.description: Brings deer and boar to the butcher and information to the captain.

npc_roster.name: Marra the Fisher
npc_roster.personality: weather-eyed, pragmatic, sings sea-shanties for fun
npc_roster.need: a new net before the spring run
npc_roster.secret: her brother runs contraband down the river by night
npc_roster.description: Pulls trout and pike from the river upstream of the mill.

npc_roster.name: Dolf the Miner
npc_roster.personality: soot-faced, superstitious, surprisingly devout
npc_roster.need: the iron seam holds out one more season
npc_roster.secret: found a vein of silver and hasn't told anyone
npc_roster.description: Works the small iron seam east of the village.

npc_roster.name: Ranna the Charcoal-Burner
npc_roster.personality: sooty, laconic, knows the woods by smell
npc_roster.need: a fresh stack of seasoned oak for the next firing
npc_roster.secret: her clamp covers a bandit cache she pretends not to see
npc_roster.description: Tends the charcoal clamps in a clearing just inside the wood.

npc_roster.name: Old Lorne
npc_roster.personality: talkative, observant, a careful collector of slights
npc_roster.need: a coin and a warm doorway
npc_roster.secret: was once a captain of horse and remembers more than he should
npc_roster.description: Sleeps in the chapel porch. Knows everyone's footstep on the cobbles.

npc_roster.name: Perrin Cup-in-Hand
npc_roster.personality: maudlin, philosophical, sometimes prophetic
npc_roster.need: one more drink, then he'll go home, honestly
npc_roster.secret: saw the magistrate's son with a shadowed-guild captain on the back road
npc_roster.description: Lives at the tavern. Has a wife somewhere who has stopped looking.

npc_roster.name: Whisper Dolf
npc_roster.personality: all charm, no conscience, lethal with a paring knife
npc_roster.need: a quiet fence for an unfortunate signet ring
npc_roster.secret: has the magistrate's seal and is waiting for the right moment
npc_roster.description: Works the market square's edges. Knows the captain's beat by heart.

npc_roster.name: Morwen of the Sunless Door
npc_roster.personality: polite, exact, terrifyingly patient
npc_roster.need: a buyer for a stolen reliquary moved fast and quiet
npc_roster.secret: is the priest's estranged sister
npc_roster.description: Runs the back room of the Sunless Door. The shadowed guild's clearing-house.

npc_roster.name: Nox the Gambler
npc_roster.personality: smooth, cheerful, never quite tells the same story twice
npc_roster.need: the mason's debt called in before the captain hears about it
npc_roster.secret: his loaded dice were a gift from a former crown captain
npc_roster.description: Holds court at the tavern's back table. Never the bank, never quite the player.

npc_roster.name: Weyland the Smuggler
npc_roster.personality: calm, well-dressed, unsettlingly courteous
npc_roster.need: the river patrol routed past Hollow Bend tonight
npc_roster.secret: has the captain's old IOU in a sealed box
npc_roster.description: Manages the shadowed guild's river runs. Smiles at the captain in the street.

npc_roster.name: Bandit Garth
npc_roster.personality: hot-headed, vain, secretly cowardly
npc_roster.need: a missing crew member returned alive
npc_roster.secret: lost a tooth to a captain's punch and now wears a wooden one he claims is enemy bone
npc_roster.description: Runs the bandit camp at Ash Hollow. The garrison would rather not say his name.

npc_roster.name: Oren the Deserter
npc_roster.personality: nervous, ashamed, surprisingly kind
npc_roster.need: a way out of the kingdom before the captain finds him
npc_roster.secret: is Guard Juno's older brother
npc_roster.description: Lives rough in the woods near the village. Watches the gate from the trees.

npc_roster.name: Seraphine of the Long Road
npc_roster.personality: quick-tongued, generous, professionally curious
npc_roster.need: a new song that will outlast her
npc_roster.secret: writes the priest's secret correspondence to his sister
npc_roster.description: Sings at the Cracked Tankard most nights. Knows every traveller's tune.

npc_roster.name: The Hermit Vey
npc_roster.personality: ancient, gentle, often silent for days
npc_roster.need: fresh candles before the long dark
npc_roster.secret: was once the abbot's closest friend
npc_roster.description: Lives in Hawthorn Hermitage past the wood. The chapel sends him bread weekly.

npc_roster.name: Thorne of the Greyhedge
npc_roster.personality: amused, blunt, profoundly unimpressed by clergy
npc_roster.need: a rare moss from the high pass that her supplier failed to bring
npc_roster.secret: saved the priest's life from a fever once and never let him forget
npc_roster.description: Lives between the village and the wild. Sells charms the priest tells you not to buy.

npc_roster.name: Corwin the Alchemist
npc_roster.personality: eccentric, hopeful, slightly singed
npc_roster.need: a clean glass alembic; the last one cracked at the south pass
npc_roster.secret: transmuted lead into something only twice in his life
npc_roster.description: Stays at the inn whenever passing through, fills his room with steam.

npc_roster.name: Solvi the Reader
npc_roster.personality: showy, observant, more honest than her customers expect
npc_roster.need: a long, dim tent and an honest customer for once
npc_roster.secret: actually has the sight (rarely)
npc_roster.description: Sets up a tent at market days. Reads the cards or your face, takes either coin.

npc_roster.name: Thorvald the Scholar
npc_roster.personality: myopic, pedantic, surprisingly fierce when crossed
npc_roster.need: permission to copy a passage from the chapel's gospel
npc_roster.secret: the diocese has a warrant out for his arrest in the next county
npc_roster.description: Travels with three crates of books and a horse that hates him.

npc_roster.name: Kael One-Eye
npc_roster.personality: soft-spoken, watchful, only takes contracts she can sleep with
npc_roster.need: a job that pays better than guarding Godric's wagon
npc_roster.secret: took a contract on someone in this village last year and didn't fulfil it
npc_roster.description: Drinks at the inn, not the tavern. Pays for everything in advance.

npc_roster.name: Ranger Ivor
npc_roster.personality: pragmatic, taciturn, sleeps with one eye on the horizon
npc_roster.need: a fresh quiver of fletcher Essa's arrows
npc_roster.secret: tracks the bandits faster than the captain knows
npc_roster.description: The garrison's eyes outside the wall. Comes in once a fortnight.

npc_roster.name: Sir Aldric
npc_roster.personality: courtly, idealistic, painfully sincere
npc_roster.need: a quest to redeem his lord's name (and his own)
npc_roster.secret: his lord disowned him; he hasn't told anyone yet
npc_roster.description: Comes through the gate every spring. Stays two days. Tips the stablehand well.

npc_roster.name: Squire Petra
npc_roster.personality: earnest, watchful, more competent than the knight she serves
npc_roster.need: a polishing kit her knight broke last week
npc_roster.secret: her knight's lord is her actual uncle and she knows the family business
npc_roster.description: Sir Aldric's squire. Fixes everything he breaks.

npc_roster.name: Pilgrim Temperance
npc_roster.personality: patient, footsore, tireless of speech
npc_roster.need: lodging at the chapel for one night
npc_roster.secret: is fleeing a different chapel several counties south
npc_roster.description: Walks the cardinal pilgrimage routes. Stops at the chapel for water and prayer.

npc_roster.name: Alenia the Midwife
npc_roster.personality: calm under pressure, salt-tongued in private
npc_roster.need: fresh linen from the tailor; the chapel won't supply enough
npc_roster.secret: delivered the magistrate's bastard last winter, he doesn't know it lived
npc_roster.description: Brings most of the village's children into the world.

npc_roster.name: Morgaine the Undertaker
npc_roster.personality: quiet, kind, never breaks bread at a wake
npc_roster.need: the carpenter to hold his coffin order
npc_roster.secret: embalmed a stranger no one claimed and keeps a record of the body
npc_roster.description: Works the cemetery and prepares the dead for the priest. Lives alone.

npc_roster.name: Errand-Boy Finn
npc_roster.personality: fast, cheeky, knows every shortcut
npc_roster.need: a copper for delivering the captain's letter
npc_roster.secret: has memorised all the shadowed guild's hand signs
npc_roster.description: Runs messages between the smithy, the tavern, and the gatehouse all day.

npc_roster.name: Griselda the Washerwoman
npc_roster.personality: cheerful, voluble, runs the village's actual rumour mill
npc_roster.need: the carpenter to fix her drying rack before the rains
npc_roster.secret: knows whose blood was on the captain's third-best tunic
npc_roster.description: Washes for the chapel, the inn, and the captain. Hears everything.

law: Move along. Or don't. Your call, but I'm watching.
State your business at the gate.
By order of the crown, mind the curfew bell tonight.
Keep that hand off your weapon while you talk to me.
outlaw: Looking for something the gates don't sell?
Quiet voice, friend. The walls have ears, and most are armed.
I don't know you. Convince me that's a problem worth fixing.
chapel: Peace be on your road, traveller.
The morning bell rings for all of us. Even you.
What burden have you brought into the chapel today?
Light a candle. The order takes coppers or honest work.
trade: Buying or browsing? Either's fine, but one pays my rent.
Fair price is whatever I say it is, and I'm in a fair mood.
Mind the apprentice — he's still learning where to stand.
Anything you don't see, I might still have. Ask.
wayfarer: Just passing through. The road north was quiet, if you're going.
Stranger to stranger — what news from your side of the gate?
I keep my coin close and my conversation closer.
If you're heading my way, two travellers walk safer than one.
wild: I'd offer you a seat but my hut wouldn't survive it.
The wood listens. Speak softly while you're inside it.
Whatever you came to ask, ask plainly. I'm short on patience.
A copper in the bowl is a fair tithe for an honest answer.
peasant: Bless you, friend. Mind the cart.
Was that you near my fence last night, or am I imagining things?
Long day. Long week. Long life.
If the priest sent you, I've already paid the tithe this fortnight.

approached: Speak quick — I've a kettle on.
complimented: Generous of you. I'll remember it.
Was there something you wanted with that?
insulted: Repeat that, slowly, where the captain can hear.
I'll forget you said that. Once.
Keep walking before I make you regret your tongue.
witnessed_crime: I saw nothing. I see less every day.
That'll be a copper for the silence.
The captain hears about that before sundown.
Run, fool. Before someone less merciful saw you.
given_gift: What do you want for it?
I'll add this to the kindness ledger.

trade: {relation_name} owes me three coppers and a debt of honour.
Don't tell {relation_name} I said this, but the work has been sloppy.
Heard {relation_name} was seen with a stranger after dark.
law: {relation_name} draws a long shadow lately. Watch your back.
If {relation_name} asks where you slept last night, lie politely.
chapel: I will pray for {relation_name}. They need it.
{relation_name} has not been at confession in too long.
outlaw: {relation_name} is on the wrong list lately. Wouldn't borrow there.
Heard {relation_name} is one bad week from a price on their head.
wayfarer: {relation_name} was telling stories at the inn. Half were even true.
Saw {relation_name} on the south road. Carrying something they shouldn't.
peasant: {relation_name} short-weighted me last market. Not forgetting it.
{relation_name} buried something past the field stones. I saw the spade.
wild: {relation_name} brought me herbs that grew where they shouldn't.
Don't repeat this, but {relation_name} watches the chapel at night.
